clinical_history: This ECG was interpreted by the computer program as showing "sinus tachycardia" with premature atrial complexes and first degree AV block. Do you agree? What is your reading?
diagnosis: The rhythm is indeed atrial fibrillation (NOT sinus tach) with a rapid ventricular response. The grossly irregular QRS response and the variable atrial waves (rate well over 300 cycles/min with a changing morphology) argue strongly against atrial flutter. (Atrial flutter with variable block also is often associated with a more periodic type of  "group beating" rather than a frankly random appearing response.) Computer readings always require careful over-read!
